zh.lifeSummary: 當Emma St Leger於大約1143年出生在英國素可瓦特林時，她的父親Reginald de St Leger30歲，母親Helisende d'Eu24歲。她於大約1155年與Hugh de Longchamp II結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1196年在英國林可爾史爾享年54歲。

zh-hans.of Wilton Castle, Herefordshire, England: 英国英格兰賀福爾維爾頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Emma St Leger于大约1143年出生在英国素可瓦特林时，她的父亲Reginald de St Leger30岁，母亲Helisende d'Eu24岁。她于大约1155年与Hugh de Longchamp II结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1196年在英国林可爾史爾享年54岁。

nameMeanings.name: St
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  French: habitational name from any of the places so called, for example in Normandy, named from a religious dedication to a (male) St. Clair.
2  North American variant spelling of   St  .   Claire  .

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

nameMeanings.name: Leger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camille, Lucien, Pierre, Yvon, Antoine, Aurele, Emile, Jacques, Marcel, Zoel, Adelard.

French (Léger) and English: from the ancient Germanic personal name Leudgari, composed of the elements leud ‘people’ and gari (from garwa) ‘ready, eager’ or gari ‘spear’ and Latinized as Leodegar(ius). The name was borne by a 7th-century Christian saint, bishop of Autun, whose fame contributed to the popularity of the name in France. See also Ledger and St. Leger .
French (Léger): nickname from léger ‘light, superficial’.

variants.name: Lege
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphie, Emile, Gaston, Henri, Leonce, Minus, Numa, Pierre, Rodolph.
German: topographic name meaning ‘swamp’.
Altered form of French Léger (see Leger ).

variants.name: Legere
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Armand, Normand, Aurele, Jacques, Adelard, Alban, Antoine, Desire, Eloi, Monique, Yvon.
French (Légère): nickname from légère, a feminine form of Léger ‘light, superficial’ (see Leger 2).
Altered form of French   Leger   1 or 2.

variants.name: Lougee
meanings: Channel Islands: altered form of Laugee, a variant of French Lauger, itself either a variant of Léger (see Leger ) or a variant, with fused definite article l', of Auger .

History: The first recorded American bearer of this surname was John Lougee from the isle of Jersey. Born Jean Laugee and by trade a knitter, he was aged c. 18 or 19 when he emigrated to the USA in the late 1600s, settling in Exeter, NH, where he became known as John Lougee, marrying Anne, the daughter of Colonel Moses Gilman.

variants.name: Ledger
meanings: English: from a Norman personal name, Leodegar, Old French Legier, of ancient Germanic origin (see Leger 1).
English: metonymic occupational name from Middle English legger ‘one who lays stone, a mason’, a derivative of Old English lecgan ‘to lay’.
Americanized form of French   Leger   1.

variants.name: St. Leger
meanings: French (Saint-Léger): habitational name from any of several places in various parts of France named Saint-Léger (from the dedication of their churches to Saint Leodegar, in French St. Léger, the martyred 7th-century bishop of Autun), or a nickname with the prefix Saint as an (ironic) attachment to the personal name or surname Léger (see Leger 1).

Haitian (also Saint-Léger): from Saint-Léger, a nickname, ornamental name, or personal name composed of the French prefix Saint and the personal name Léger (see Leger 1), or perhaps a habitational name from a place in Haiti called Saint-Léger (compare 1 above).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Saint-Léger in La Manche or Saint-Léger-aux-Bois in Seine-Maritime, France (see 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: Emma
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, originally a short form of compound names such as Ermintrude containing the word erm(en), irm(en) ‘entire’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, but its popularity in medieval England was greatly enhanced by the fact that it had been borne by the mother of Edward the Confessor, herself a Norman. In modern times, it was only in moderate use early in the 20th century but rose sharply in favour in the 1970s and has since remained perennially popular.

en.lifeSummary: When Emma St Leger was born about 1143, in Wartling, Sussex, England, her father, Reginald de St Leger, was 30 and her mother, Helisende d'Eu, was 24. She married Hugh de Longchamp II about 1155. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1196, in Lincolnshire, England, at the age of 54.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Emma St Leger est née environ 1143, à Wartling, Sussex, Angleterre, son père, Reginald de St Leger, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Helisende d'Eu, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Hugh de Longchamp II environ 1155. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1196, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 54 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Emma St Leger um 1143 in Wartling, Sussex, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Reginald de St Leger 30 und ihre Mutter Helisende d'Eu 24. Sie hat um 1155 Hugh de Longchamp II geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1196 im Alter von 54 Jahren in Lincolnshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Emma St Leger è nata il circa 1143 nel Wartling, Sussex, England, sua padre, Reginald de St Leger, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, Helisende d'Eu, aveva 24 anni. Lei sposato Hugh de Longchamp II circa 1155. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1196, nel Lincolnshire, England, all'età di 54 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Emma St Legerが約1143年にイギリススセックスワートリングで生まれた時，父Reginald de St Legerは30歳，母Helisende d'Euは24歳であった。約1155年にHugh de Longchamp IIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1196年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアで54歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Emma St Leger(이)가 대략 1143년 영국 수세스 워틀링에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Reginald de St Leger(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Helisende d'Eu(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 대략 1155년 Hugh de Longchamp II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1196년 영국 링코실르에서 54세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Emma St Leger nasceu aproximadamente 1143, em Wartling, Sussex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Reginald de St Leger, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Helisende d'Eu, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com Hugh de Longchamp II aproximadamente 1155. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1196, em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, com 54 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Emma St Leger родилась около 1143 г. в местности вартлинг, суссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Reginald de St Leger было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Helisende d'Eu, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hugh de Longchamp II около 1155 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1196 г. в местности линколншир, Англия в возрасте 54 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Emma St Leger nació aproximadamente 1143, en Wartling, Sussex, Inglaterra, su papá, Reginald de St Leger, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Helisende d'Eu, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Hugh de Longchamp II aproximadamente 1155. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1196, en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 54 años.
